last_completed_round.direction_name: Retry PTX Hot-Band Grouping With A 4-Row Window
last_completed_round.hypothesis: Round 10/17 closed the non-PTX 128x128 sibling decisively: it preserved correctness but blew DRAM up to 34.17 and regressed runtime to 26.70438385 ms, so the path away from the PTX microkernel is not competitive. That pushes the search back to the surviving PTX-adjacent levers on top of the accepted export base. The prior grouped-row retune to 5 was measured before the new export cleanup became the accepted baseline. Reopening the grouping family with a 4-row window is the next best bounded move because it keeps the proven PTX/export path intact while retesting CTA orchestration with a more symmetric grouping size than 5.

notes: Build passed for round 12/17. Node A will measure the result next.
